mission: AKIDA AKD1000 intrinsic spike-noise vs ANU QRNG quantum-vacuum noise — KS / KL / NIST distinguishability

phases.name: AKD1000 boot + Edge Impulse demo flash
phases.method: stock vendor demo: keyword-spotting MetaTF model end-to-end

phases.name: AKIDA constant-input ISI harvest
phases.method: single-NPU integrate-and-fire, DC bias slightly above threshold; capture spike-event log; compute inter-spike-interval sequence n=1e5

phases.name: ANU QRNG sample harvest
phases.method: n_bytes=1e5 in 1024-byte chunks, 1000 ms inter-chunk pause; ANIMA_RNG_SOURCE=anu strict mode

phases.name: Statistical comparison battery
phases.method: KS 2-sample (scipy-equivalent in HEXA), KL divergence binned 256, NIST SP 800-22 monobit + runs + serial-3

phases.name: Graduation row to atlas_convergence_witness
phases.method: if INDISTINGUISHABLE_HEADLINE or STRUCTURED_HEADLINE, append JSONL row layer=n_substrate_n7_spike_qrng
phases.blocker: F-N7 fully evaluated, verdict in {INDISTINGUISHABLE_HEADLINE, STRUCTURED_HEADLINE}

honest_c3_critical_gaps: BrainChip does NOT publish per-event jitter / Vth-sigma datasheet figures
1.67 ns sigma_jitter and 1% Vth-sigma are pre-arrival synthesized priors, F-N7-E catches divergence at D+0
ANU API effective rate ~1 kbit/s (not 8192 bit/s); 13 min wall-clock per 1e5-byte harvest
ANU stream has community-pass NIST tests but no vendor SP 800-22 certification (we run T3 ourselves)
ANU-to-ISI remap is matched-mean shape comparison, not raw rate comparison
